journal_entry.description: Recognize revenue for service delivered against customer deposit
journal_entry.rationale: Customer previously paid a $100 deposit, which was recorded as Unearned Revenue (a liability). Now that the service has been delivered, we must decrease the liability (debit Unearned Revenue) and recognize the earned revenue (credit Service Revenue or similar revenue account). This follows the IFRS 15 revenue recognition principle: revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. No cash changes hands in this transaction since payment was received earlier. No tax lines are needed as the tax would have been handled when the deposit was originally received.

output.reason: The customer deposit was previously recorded as a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, the deposit liability is discharged. Debit: liability decrease (customer deposit/unearned revenue).

debit_classifier.reason: The customer deposit was previously recorded as a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, the deposit liability is discharged. Debit: liability decrease (customer deposit/unearned revenue).

output.reason: This transaction involves delivering service that was previously paid for as a deposit. The credit side has one line: recognizing revenue for the service delivered (revenue increase). The customer deposit liability is reduced (debit side, not classified here).

credit_classifier.reason: This transaction involves delivering service that was previously paid for as a deposit. The credit side has one line: recognizing revenue for the service delivered (revenue increase). The customer deposit liability is reduced (debit side, not classified here).
